heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Dennis Hopper. Written by Dennis Hopper, Peter Fonda, and Terry Southern.
clean: Directed by Dennis Hopper. Written by Dennis Hopper, Peter Fonda, and Terry Southern.

heading: Wyatt
raw: No, I mean it. You've got a nice place. It's not every man that can live off the land, you know. You do your own thing in your own time. You should be proud.
clean: No, I mean it. You've got a nice place. It's not every man that can live off the land, you know. You do your own thing in your own time. You should be proud.

heading: Wyatt
raw: [reading inscription] "If God did not exist, it would be necessary to invent Him."
clean: If God did not exist, it would be necessary to invent Him.

heading: Wyatt
raw: [while tripping on LSD] Oh Mother why didn't you tell me? Why didn't anybody tell me anything?...What are you doing to me now?...Shut up!...How could you make me hate you so?...Oh God, I hate you so much.
clean: Oh Mother why didn't you tell me? Why didn't anybody tell me anything?...What are you doing to me now?...Shut up!...How could you make me hate you so?...Oh God, I hate you so much.

heading: Wyatt

heading: Billy
raw: I never really thought of myself as a freak, ya know...but I Love to Freak!
clean: I never really thought of myself as a freak, ya know...but I Love to Freak!

heading: Billy
raw: Whew. Man, look, I gotta get out of here, man. Now we - we got things we want to do, man, like - I just - uh - I gotta get out of here, man.
clean: Whew. Man, look, I gotta get out of here, man. Now we - we got things we want to do, man, like - I just - uh - I gotta get out of here, man.

heading: Billy
raw: You gotta be kidding. I mean, you know who this is, man? This is Captain America. I'm Billy. Hey, we're headliners baby. We played every fair in this part of the country. I mean, for top dollar, too!
clean: You gotta be kidding. I mean, you know who this is, man? This is Captain America. I'm Billy. Hey, we're headliners baby. We played every fair in this part of the country. I mean, for top dollar, too!

heading: George Hanson
raw: You can get out of here, if you haven't killed anybody - at least nobody white.
clean: You can get out of here, if you haven't killed anybody - at least nobody white.

heading: George Hanson
raw: [to guard] Very groovy. Very groovy. See there. I bet nobody ever said that to you.
clean: Very groovy. Very groovy. See there. I bet nobody ever said that to you.

heading: George Hanson
raw: Here's to the first of the day, fellas. To ol' D. H. Lawrence. Nik-nik-nik-f-f-f-Indians!
clean: Here's to the first of the day, fellas. To ol' D. H. Lawrence. Nik-nik-nik-f-f-f-Indians!

heading: George Hanson
raw: I must've started off to Mardi Gras six or seven times. Never got further than the state line.
clean: I must've started off to Mardi Gras six or seven times. Never got further than the state line.

heading: George Hanson
raw: 'Madame Tinkertoy's House of Blue Lights. Corner of Bourbon and Toulouse, New Orleans, Louisiana.' Now this is supposed to be the finest whorehouse in the South. These ain't no pork chops. These are U.S. Prime.
clean: 'Madame Tinkertoy's House of Blue Lights. Corner of Bourbon and Toulouse, New Orleans, Louisiana.' Now this is supposed to be the finest whorehouse in the South. These ain't no pork chops. These are U.S. Prime.

heading: George Hanson
raw: You- you mean marijuana. Lord have mercy, is that what that is? Well, let me see that. Mmmmm-mmm. Mmmm...I-I-I couldn't do that. I mean, I've got enough problems with the - with the booze and all. I mean, uh, I - I can't afford to get hooked...it-it-it leads to harder stuff.

heading: The Stranger
raw: It doesn't make any difference what city. All cities are alike. That's why I'm out here now...cause I'm from the city, a long way from the city - and that's where I want to be right now.
clean: It doesn't make any difference what city. All cities are alike. That's why I'm out here now...cause I'm from the city, a long way from the city - and that's where I want to be right now.

heading: The Stranger
raw: The people this place belongs to are buried right under you. You could be a trifle polite...It's a small thing to ask.
clean: The people this place belongs to are buried right under you. You could be a trifle polite...It's a small thing to ask.

heading: Others
raw: Rancher: Turn that thing off. You're making my horse skittish. ... Sure is a good-looking machine.
clean: Turn that thing off. You're making my horse skittish. ... Sure is a good-looking machine.

heading: Others
raw: Joanne: [reading from the I-Ching] Starting brings misfortune. Per-serverance brings danger. Not every demand for change in the existing order should be heeded. On the other hand, repeated and well-founded complaints should not fail to a hearing.
clean: Starting brings misfortune. Per-serverance brings danger. Not every demand for change in the existing order should be heeded. On the other hand, repeated and well-founded complaints should not fail to a hearing.

heading: Others
raw: The Devil: Hear ye, hear ye, hear ye. We've come to play for our dinner. Or should I say, stay for our dinner. Or even slay for our dinner...We've come to drink your wine, taste your food and take pleasure in your women.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Billy: You're pulling inside, man. You're getting a little distance tonight.
clean: You're pulling inside, man. You're getting a little distance tonight.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Wyatt: Yeah, well, I'm just getting my thing together.
clean: Yeah, well, I'm just getting my thing together.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Billy: Hey man, everything that we ever dreamed of is in that teardrop gas tank - and you got a stranger over there pourin' gasoline all over it. Man, all he's got to do is turn and look over into it, man, and he can see that...
clean: Hey man, everything that we ever dreamed of is in that teardrop gas tank - and you got a stranger over there pourin' gasoline all over it. Man, all he's got to do is turn and look over into it, man, and he can see that...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Wyatt: He won't know what it is, man. He won't know what it is. Don't worry, Billy. Everything's all right.
clean: He won't know what it is, man. He won't know what it is. Don't worry, Billy. Everything's all right.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Wyatt: You ever want to be somebody else?
clean: You ever want to be somebody else?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Stranger: I'd like to try Porky Pig.
clean: I'd like to try Porky Pig.

heading: Dialogue

clean: I never wanted to be anybody else.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Billy: This is nothing but sand, man. They ain't gonna make it, man. They ain't gonna grow anything here.
clean: This is nothing but sand, man. They ain't gonna make it, man. They ain't gonna grow anything here.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Wyatt: They're gonna make it. Dig, man. They're gonna make it.
clean: They're gonna make it. Dig, man. They're gonna make it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Stranger: [giving Wyatt some LSD] When you get to the right place, with the right people, quarter this. You know, this could be the right place. The time's running out.
clean: When you get to the right place, with the right people, quarter this. You know, this could be the right place. The time's running out.

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

clean: I'm sorry about the misunderstanding.

heading: Dialogue

clean: There was no misunderstanding. We're all in the same cage here.

heading: Dialogue

clean: You must be some important dude. That treatment--

heading: Dialogue

clean: What does he mean "dude"? Dude ranch?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Wyatt: "Dude" means a nice guy, you know? "Dude" means a regular sort of person.
clean: "Dude" means a nice guy, you know? "Dude" means a regular sort of person.

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

clean: Check that joker with the long hair.

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue
raw: Deputy: Isn't she, though. I guess we'd put him in the women's cell, don't you reckon?

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue
raw: George: Those are what is known as 'country witticisms'.
clean: Those are what is known as 'country witticisms'.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Customer 1: You know, I thought at first that bunch over there, their mothers had maybe been frightened by a bunch of gorillas, but now I think they were caught.

heading: Dialogue

clean: Customer 2: I know one of them's Alley-oop - I think. From the beads on him.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Customer 4: Well, one of them darned sure is not Oola.
clean: Customer 4: Well, one of them darned sure is not Oola.

heading: Dialogue

clean: Customer 1: Look like a bunch of refugees from a gorilla love-in.

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue
raw: Customer 4: Oh, now I don't know about that.

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue
raw: Billy: Huh. Man, everybody got chicken, that's what happened, man. Hey, we can't even get into like, uh, second-rate hotel, I mean, a second-rate motel. You dig? They think we're gonna cut their throat or something, man. They're scared, man.

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue

heading: Dialogue
